stories.title: Trump departs for Beijing, says U.S. needs no help to beat Iran
stories.description: The president dismissed the need for Chinese intervention in the two-month-old Iran war as he left for a summit with Xi Jinping.

stories.title: Altman testifies Musk sought control of OpenAI before departure
stories.description: The OpenAI CEO told jurors Musk wanted to merge the AI startup with Tesla and pushed for long-term dominance over the organization.

stories.title: WHO raises hantavirus cruise ship cases to 11, warns more likely
stories.description: Spain confirmed a new infection among repatriated passengers as WHO said the virus's long incubation period means additional cases are expected.

stories.title: CBO pegs Trump's Golden Dome at $1.2 trillion over 20 years
stories.description: The estimate is roughly seven times the $175 billion price tag Trump cited when he unveiled the missile defense program last year.

stories.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum rules out US extraction of indicted Sinaloa governor
stories.description: The president said U.S. authorities will not be allowed to forcibly remove Governor Rocha Moya, who faces federal drug trafficking charges.

stories.title: Starmer tells cabinet he won't quit as rivals circle
stories.description: The PM challenged Labour critics to formally trigger a leadership contest, as Burnham, Rayner and Streeting jostle to succeed him.

stories.title: Makary resigns as FDA commissioner; Diamantas named acting chief
stories.description: HHS Secretary Kennedy made the final call after months of clashes over vaping, abortion policy, and drug approvals, according to Politico.

stories.title: Pentagon raises Iran war cost estimate to $29 billion
stories.description: The updated figure marks a $4 billion jump from two weeks ago, though independent analysts say the true cost is far higher.

stories.title: US consumer prices jump 3.8% in April, fastest pace since mid-2023
stories.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that annual inflation accelerated from 3.3% in March, driven by the Iran war's ongoing energy shock.

stories.title: Senate confirms Warsh to Fed board, chair vote next
stories.description: The Tuesday vote completes the first step toward installing Trump's pick as Fed chair before Jerome Powell's term expires Friday.

stories.title: Iran redefines Strait of Hormuz as 500-km military zone
stories.description: The IRGC Navy says its operational area now spans a "complete crescent" from Jask to beyond Qeshm Island, a tenfold expansion of the critical oil chokepoint.

stories.title: Russia launches over 200 drones as U.S.-brokered ceasefire expires
stories.description: Ukraine says Russia struck civilian targets including a Kyiv high-rise and a kindergarten overnight, killing at least one and wounding several.

stories.title: Trump says Cuba is 'asking for help,' signals talks ahead of China trip
stories.description: The president posted on Truth Social that the U.S. will engage in discussions with Havana, without offering details on timing or format.

stories.title: Navy's 30-year shipbuilding plan opens door to overseas construction
stories.description: The newly released strategy aims for 450 vessels by 2031 and seeks congressional authority to build some ships at allied shipyards abroad.

stories.title: Pakistan calls CBS report on Iranian jets at airbase 'misleading'
stories.description: The Foreign Office dismissed claims that Iran parked military aircraft at Nur Khan Airbase to shield them from U.S. strikes, citing routine diplomatic logistics.

stories.title: China pressed Anthropic for access to its Mythos AI model and was refused
stories.description: The approach at a Singapore meeting alarmed White House officials ahead of the Trump-Xi summit set for May 14-15 in Beijing.

stories.title: Lebanon urges US pressure on Israel to halt raids as ceasefire frays
stories.description: President Aoun and PM Salam pressed the U.S. ambassador to push Israel to stop strikes ahead of Washington talks this week.

stories.title: CENTCOM says 62 ships redirected under Iran blockade
stories.description: More than 20 U.S. warships are enforcing the naval blockade as Iran submits a 14-point counter-proposal to end the standoff.

stories.title: Trump delays plan to suspend beef import tariffs
stories.description: The administration postponed executive orders that would have waived tariff-rate quotas on beef for 200 days to help lower record-high prices.

stories.title: Brazilian flotilla activist returns home alleging torture by Israel
stories.description: Thiago Ávila arrived in São Paulo on Monday after 10 days in Israeli custody, claiming he and other detainees suffered widespread abuses.

stories.title: Turkey set to receive first-ever US emergency oil shipment
stories.description: Two tankers carrying nearly 1.8 million barrels of Strategic Petroleum Reserve crude are headed to Turkey amid the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

stories.title: Navy confirms Trump-class battleships will be nuclear-powered
stories.description: A new 30-year shipbuilding plan also reveals the Navy intends to buy 15 of the vessels, up from three previously announced.

stories.title: Japan pushes for New Zealand to choose Mogami-class frigates over UK rival
stories.description: Tokyo voiced hope Monday that New Zealand will select the upgraded Mogami-class frigate, which Australia has already ordered, over Britain's Type 31.

stories.title: Iran says 35 million people face water shortages as crises mount
stories.description: Officials warned Monday that drought, a U.S. naval blockade, mass layoffs, and an internet blackout are converging into a deepening domestic emergency.

stories.title: Trump weighs military action as Iran ceasefire talks collapse
stories.description: The president convened his national security team Monday after rejecting Tehran's latest proposal and declaring the truce on "massive life support."

stories.title: Democrats demand Kiggans resign over racial slur response
stories.description: The Congressional Black Caucus and Democratic lawmakers called for her ouster after she agreed with a host's racist remark about Hakeem Jeffries.

stories.title: Pakistan let Iran park warplanes on its airfields
stories.description: Islamabad quietly hosted Iranian military aircraft at Nur Khan Airbase while serving as the central mediator between Washington and Tehran.

stories.title: Pentagon reveals nuclear submarine's location in rare message to Iran
stories.description: The rare public deployment of a nuclear-armed submarine coincides with collapsed peace talks and Trump's rejection of Tehran's counterproposal.

stories.title: House Democrats demand subpoenas over prediction market insider trading
stories.description: Seven lawmakers asked Oversight Chair Comer to investigate suspicious Polymarket bets placed hours before U.S. strikes on Iran.

stories.title: IDF sentences soldiers for desecrating Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
stories.description: The sentencing marks the second punishment in weeks for vandalism of Christian symbols in the same southern Lebanese village.

stories.title: Trump to undergo annual physical May 26 at Walter Reed
stories.description: The White House announced the 79-year-old president's dental and medical evaluations amid months of public speculation about his health.

stories.title: Kennedy quietly leading broad vaccine safety probe across health agencies
stories.description: While publicly silent on vaccines ahead of midterms, the health secretary is directing government scientists to investigate his theories on vaccines and chronic disease.

stories.title: EU's Kallas says Putin is 'weaker than ever' on Ukraine
stories.description: The bloc also imposed new sanctions on 16 individuals and seven entities tied to the forced relocation of an estimated 20,500 Ukrainian children.

stories.title: GOP splits over $1B security provision for Trump ballroom
stories.description: Senate Republicans returned to Washington Monday facing internal resistance to a $1 billion taxpayer-funded security provision tied to Trump's White House ballroom project.

stories.title: California mayor agrees to plead guilty to acting as Chinese agent
stories.description: Eileen Wang resigned and will plead guilty to secretly promoting China's interests through a propaganda website with her then-fiancé.

stories.title: Knesset passes law creating military tribunal for Oct. 7 suspects
stories.description: The bipartisan legislation establishes a special court in Jerusalem to try hundreds of detained Hamas attackers, with genocide convictions carrying a mandatory death sentence.

stories.title: Trump urges House to pass housing bill banning corporate homebuyers
stories.description: The president demanded Congress approve the Senate's 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which restricts large investors from buying single-family homes.

stories.title: House Ethics Committee issues 20-plus subpoenas in Rep. Cory Mills probe
stories.description: The panel called allegations of dating violence, campaign finance violations, and military service misrepresentation "serious and complex."

stories.title: Supreme Court clears Alabama to drop a majority-Black House district
stories.description: The justices vacated a lower court order requiring two majority-Black congressional districts, sending the case back under a new, stricter legal standard.

stories.title: Trump nominates Kari Lake as US ambassador to Jamaica
stories.description: The nomination moves Lake out of her role at USAGM, where a federal judge ruled her appointment was unlawful and voided her actions.

stories.title: MSF suspends operations in Haiti's Cité Soleil as gang clashes force hospital evacuations
stories.description: The only trauma hospital in the densely populated Port-au-Prince district shut down Monday, leaving hundreds of thousands without medical care.

stories.title: Virginia Democrats ask Supreme Court to restore voter-approved congressional map
stories.description: The emergency appeal seeks to reverse a state court ruling that voided an April redistricting referendum projected to give Democrats four additional House seats.

stories.title: Comey calls indictment evidence of Trump's 'bottomless desire' for revenge
stories.description: The former FBI director spoke out Monday as his July trial on charges tied to a seashell Instagram post approaches.

stories.title: Putin says war 'coming to an end' as Germany rejects his peace mediator
stories.description: Germany dismissed Putin's proposal to use ex-Chancellor Schröder as a mediator, saying Russia has shown no signs of serious interest in negotiations.

stories.title: Suspect in White House dinner shooting pleads not guilty as poll reveals widespread doubt
stories.description: Cole Allen entered a not guilty plea Monday on charges including attempted assassination of the president, as a new survey found 24% of Americans believe the attack was staged.

stories.title: Trump says he's 'seriously considering' Venezuela as 51st state
stories.description: Venezuela's acting president rejected the proposal, saying "that idea would never have crossed our minds" while speaking at The Hague.

stories.title: Iran threatens 'decisive' response to UK, French warships near Hormuz
stories.description: Tehran warned Sunday that European naval vessels entering the Strait of Hormuz would be met with force, as Macron said the mission would be "coordinated with Iran."

stories.title: Supreme Court extends abortion pill mail access until Thursday
stories.description: The justices now have until May 14 to decide whether to allow a Fifth Circuit ruling reinstating in-person dispensing requirements to take effect nationwide.

stories.title: Two-thirds of Americans say Trump hasn't justified Iran war, poll finds
stories.description: A Reuters/Ipsos survey completed Monday also found 63% of Americans say surging gas prices from the conflict have hurt their finances.

stories.title: Hassett rules out new agency for AI oversight
stories.description: The statement walks back a week of speculation after the White House floated FDA-style pre-approval for AI models.

stories.title: Iran's record internet blackout threatens mass layoffs as economic toll mounts
stories.description: Bloomberg reports the 70-day-plus shutdown, the longest ever recorded in a connected society, is devastating private businesses alongside war damage.

stories.title: Trump administration repeals Biden-era conservation rule for public lands
stories.description: The BLM formally struck down a rule that had elevated habitat protection alongside drilling and grazing on 245 million acres in the West.

stories.title: One Nation wins first federal lower house seat in Farrer by-election
stories.description: David Farley's emphatic victory ends the Coalition's 77-year grip on the NSW electorate, marking a historic breakthrough for Pauline Hanson's party.

stories.title: Global oil stockpiles draining at record pace amid Iran war
stories.description: Morgan Stanley estimates inventories fell by 4.8 million barrels a day from March to late April, the steepest drawdown the IEA has ever recorded.

stories.title: DOJ launches aggressive leak probes after Trump presses AG on Iran war coverage
stories.description: The Wall Street Journal reports Trump privately pushed acting AG Todd Blanche to crack down on media leaks tied to the Iran conflict.

stories.title: Israel built secret base in Iraq during Iran war, WSJ reports
stories.description: The clandestine outpost housed special forces and served as a logistics hub, with U.S. knowledge but without Iraq's approval.

stories.title: U.S. strikes third alleged drug boat in five days, killing 2
stories.description: The campaign known as Operation Southern Spear has now killed at least 193 people since September, according to the AP.

stories.title: Trump nominates fired FEMA chief Hamilton to lead agency again
stories.description: The former Navy SEAL was dismissed a year ago after telling Congress that abolishing FEMA was not in the country's best interests.

stories.title: Bahrain arrests 41 over alleged links to Iran's IRGC
stories.description: The detainees are accused of espionage and sympathy with Iranian aggression, part of a broader crackdown since the U.S.-Israel-Iran war began.

stories.title: Alito relied on misleading DOJ data to gut Voting Rights Act, analysis finds
stories.description: The Guardian found the voter turnout statistics in the Supreme Court's landmark Section 2 ruling used a methodology experts call deliberately deceptive.

stories.title: Trump says he'll raise Taiwan arms sales with Xi in Beijing
stories.description: A bipartisan group of senators is pressing him to advance a stalled $14 billion weapons package before the May 14-15 summit.

stories.title: US restates opposition to West Bank annexation at UN
stories.description: Ambassador Jeff Bartos told a UN Security Council session that Washington opposes Israeli annexation, citing regional stability concerns.

stories.title: Rubio calls Iran's claim over Strait of Hormuz 'unacceptable' as U.S. awaits deal response
stories.description: The secretary of state warned from Rome that Tehran's new transit authority over the critical waterway sets a dangerous precedent.

stories.title: Over 2,500 scientists urge Congress to reverse Trump's NSF board firings
stories.description: An open letter calls the dismissal of all 22 National Science Board members an "alarming attack" on U.S. research funding and scientific independence.

stories.title: Four men found guilty in assassination of Haitian President Moïse
stories.description: A Miami federal jury convicted four men on all counts for conspiring to kill the Haitian leader, who was gunned down in 2021.

stories.title: Pentagon releases 162 declassified UFO files to public
stories.description: The first batch includes unresolved cases, Apollo mission photos, and infrared imagery, all accessible without security clearance at war.gov/UFO.

stories.title: Trump says he plans to suspend federal gas tax
stories.description: The president confirmed the move Monday, one day after Energy Secretary Wright said the administration was "open to all ideas" on fuel prices.

stories.title: US adds 115,000 jobs in April, nearly doubling forecasts
stories.description: The unemployment rate held at 4.3% as the economy posted back-to-back solid gains after months of volatile swings between job losses and gains.

stories.title: Reform UK surges as Labour loses hundreds of seats in local elections
stories.description: Starmer vowed he would "not walk away" as Labour lost control of at least eight councils and more than 235 seats in early English results.

stories.title: Duffy defends reality TV show amid backlash
stories.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy faces bipartisan backlash over a corporate-sponsored family road trip series filmed as fuel costs surged more than 50%.

stories.title: Taiwan passes $25B defense bill for US arms purchases
stories.description: The opposition-led legislature approved far less than the $40 billion the government sought, excluding domestically built drones and missile defenses.

stories.title: Kremlin drafts plan to sell peace deal as victory, leaked documents show
stories.description: Internal strategy documents task Russia's political bloc with reframing the war's end as a triumph amid economic strain and public fatigue.

stories.title: Trump calls on Supreme Court justices to be 'loyal' to him on key rulings
stories.description: The president singled out his own appointees Gorsuch and Barrett after a string of court defeats on tariffs and ahead of a birthright citizenship ruling.

stories.title: Iran warns UAE will 'pay the price' after Qeshm blasts
stories.description: IRGC-linked media accused Abu Dhabi of hostile action after explosions hit Iran's southern coast, while Tehran also alleged U.S. ceasefire violations.

stories.title: US to start revoking passports of parents over unpaid child support
stories.description: The State Department will begin revoking passports Friday for parents owing $100,000 or more, with plans to expand to a $2,500 threshold.

stories.title: Biden to fight release of 70 hours of ghostwriter audio
stories.description: A spokesperson confirmed Sunday that the former president will go to court to block the Trump DOJ from disclosing recordings tied to a classified documents probe.

stories.title: CIA says Iran can withstand U.S. blockade for months
stories.description: A classified assessment finds Iran retains enough economic buffers and military capability to endure the naval blockade for 90 to 120 days, the Washington Post reports.

stories.title: New York reaches $268B budget deal with second-home tax
stories.description: The agreement, announced five weeks past its April 1 deadline, includes a pied-à-terre levy on luxury homes and limits on ICE cooperation.

stories.title: Trump eyes TikTok settlement funds for D.C. beautification projects
stories.description: The administration is nearing a $400 million deal to resolve a child privacy lawsuit, with proceeds directed toward Washington improvements instead of victims.

stories.title: ICE drops fast-tracked training, restores 72-day program
stories.description: The reversal follows months of bipartisan scrutiny, whistleblower testimony, and a behind-the-scenes "handshake deal" with lawmakers during shutdown negotiations.

stories.title: Judge unseals alleged Epstein suicide note after 7 years
stories.description: The handwritten note, found by a cellmate weeks before Epstein's 2019 death, had been sealed in a courthouse vault and never shared with federal investigators.

stories.title: US sanctions Chinese satellite firms over Iran war
stories.description: Washington targeted three companies accused of providing imagery that enabled Iranian strikes on American forces, days before Trump's Beijing summit.

stories.title: 8 in 10 Americans back age caps for Congress, poll finds
stories.description: The NPR/PBS News/Marist poll found bipartisan support for both age limits and term limits, with Gen X expressing the strongest backing.

stories.title: North Korea declares it is not bound by nuclear treaty
stories.description: Pyongyang's UN envoy rejected the NPT at the ongoing review conference in New York, reaffirming the country's stance as a nuclear weapons state.

stories.title: Judge strikes down DOGE's ChatGPT-led mass termination of NEH grants
stories.description: The 143-page ruling found the ChatGPT-assisted cancellation of over 1,400 humanities grants violated the First and Fifth Amendments.

stories.title: Ted Turner, CNN founder and media mogul, dies at 87
stories.description: Turner, who had been living with Lewy body dementia since 2018, died Wednesday at his home near Tallahassee, Florida.

stories.title: Iran's Ghalibaf vows nation 'will not surrender' amid US naval blockade
stories.description: Parliament Speaker called for austerity and unity as the rial hits record lows and inflation surpasses 65% under the three-week-old siege.

stories.title: Trump gives EU until July 4 to ratify trade deal
stories.description: The president set the deadline after overnight trilogue talks on the Turnberry Agreement collapsed without a breakthrough.

stories.title: North Korea drops reunification from constitution, codifies Kim's nuclear authority
stories.description: The revised charter defines North Korean territory as bordering South Korea and formally places command of the nuclear arsenal in Kim Jong Un's hands.

stories.title: China's Big Fund in talks to lead DeepSeek round at $45B valuation
stories.description: The state-backed semiconductor fund's potential investment in the AI lab signals Beijing now views model capability as a strategic priority.

stories.title: Trump Minneapolis immigration blitz hobbled gun, drug cases, Reuters finds
stories.description: Federal criminal prosecutions in Minnesota fell sharply as Operation Metro Surge diverted thousands of agents from drug and gun investigations.

stories.title: Democrat Greene wins Michigan Senate special election
stories.description: The victory preserves Democrats' narrow state Senate majority during Gov. Whitmer's final months in office.

stories.title: Rubio calls Israel-Lebanon peace 'imminently achievable'
stories.description: The secretary of state identified Hezbollah as the sole obstacle to a deal, speaking at a White House briefing on Tuesday.

stories.title: Boeing CEO expected to join Trump on Beijing trip next week
stories.description: The planemaker is seeking its first major Chinese order in nearly a decade at the May 14-15 summit with President Xi Jinping.

stories.title: Ramaswamy wins Ohio GOP primary, setting up costly governor's race
stories.description: The Trump-backed entrepreneur will face Democrat Amy Acton in November after cruising past longshot challenger Casey Putsch on Tuesday.

stories.title: US military confirms loss of KC-135 tanker over Persian Gulf
stories.description: The refueling aircraft broadcast a 7700 distress signal and vanished from radar during Operation Project Freedom, the effort to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Rubio says Taiwan will come up at Trump-Xi summit next week
stories.description: The secretary of state said neither side wants instability in the Indo-Pacific ahead of Trump's May 14-15 visit to Beijing.

stories.title: Apple in talks with Intel, Samsung to make device chips in the US
stories.description: The discussions could end a decade-long exclusive chipmaking partnership with TSMC as AI-driven supply constraints squeeze Apple's access to fabrication capacity.

stories.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani skips Met Gala, spotlights garment workers instead
stories.description: Rather than attending fashion's biggest night, New York's mayor published portraits of garment district workers whose labor makes the industry possible.

stories.title: Judge lets Trump DOJ keep seized Fulton County ballots
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Boulee ruled the FBI can retain more than 600 boxes of 2020 election materials taken in a January raid.

stories.title: RFK Jr launches plan to curb antidepressant prescriptions
stories.description: Kennedy unveiled the MAHA action plan at a Monday summit, targeting SSRI overprescribing with new clinician guidance and deprescribing reimbursements.

stories.title: Iran's nuclear timeline unchanged despite war, US intel finds
stories.description: Tehran can still build a bomb in about a year, the same estimate as last summer, despite two months of U.S. military operations, according to Reuters.

stories.title: Trump-backed challengers oust most Indiana GOP senators who blocked redistricting
stories.description: A majority of Republican state senators targeted by Trump over their December vote against his congressional redistricting push lost their primaries Tuesday.

stories.title: ACLU files suit to block Texas migrant arrest law
stories.description: The class-action lawsuit seeks emergency relief against SB 4 provisions that would let state police and judges enforce immigration law.

stories.title: Putin retreating to bunkers amid coup and drone fears, EU intelligence report says
stories.description: A drone struck a Moscow high-rise just 6 km from the Kremlin overnight, days before Russia's Victory Day parade.

stories.title: FDA blocked studies finding Covid, shingles vaccines safe
stories.description: Agency scientists were told to retract approved Covid vaccine safety studies and barred from presenting shingles research at a conference, the Times reports.

stories.title: GameStop launches $56 billion bid to acquire eBay
stories.description: CEO Ryan Cohen told The Wall Street Journal that GameStop has built a 5% stake in eBay and secured $20 billion in debt financing from TD Bank.

stories.title: NATO's Rutte says Europeans 'got the message' on Iran war
stories.description: The NATO chief spoke Monday after the U.S. announced it would pull 5,000 troops from Germany over allies' reluctance to support the Iran campaign.

stories.title: Trump administration weighs pre-release vetting of AI models
stories.description: The potential policy would reverse the president's hands-off approach to AI regulation, spurred by national security fears over Anthropic's Mythos model.

stories.title: Starmer, Zelenskyy meet in Yerevan as UK seeks to join EU's Ukraine loan program
stories.description: The British prime minister is pushing for UK defense firms to compete for contracts under the EU's newly approved €90 billion loan for Kyiv.

stories.title: Competitive House seats hit historic low amid map wars
stories.description: Just 16 tossup districts will likely decide control of the House, the fewest since Cook Political Report began ratings in 1984.

stories.title: Trump's 'Project Freedom' launches with few details, leaving shippers scrambling
stories.description: The U.S. began guiding vessels through the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, but shipping executives say the plan lacks coordination and clarity.

stories.title: Bipartisan bill would cut university funds over foreign ties
stories.description: Lawmakers introduced legislation targeting colleges with branch campuses or research ties to China, Qatar, Iran, Russia, and other adversarial nations.

stories.title: Zelenskyy flags 'specific activity' on Belarus border
stories.description: Ukraine detected unusual movements on the Belarusian side of its northern border on May 1, with Zelenskyy vowing to respond if needed.

stories.title: DHS lifts visa processing hold for foreign doctors under travel ban
stories.description: USCIS updated its website to restore visa processing for physicians from 39 banned countries after hospitals placed doctors on leave.

stories.title: 2 US service members missing after exercise in Morocco
stories.description: AFRICOM said the pair went missing Saturday near the Cap Draa Training Area during African Lion 2026, the command's largest annual joint exercise.

stories.title: Democrats need four Senate flips in deep-red states
stories.description: NPR's latest midterm analysis ranks North Carolina as the most likely flip, with Alaska as the potential majority-maker for either party.

stories.title: Iran war gave China, Russia real-time look at US military gaps, WSJ reports
stories.description: The conflict exposed American defense vulnerabilities, from THAAD radar disruptions to rapid depletion of critical missile stockpiles.

stories.title: Melania Trump pushed to cancel dinner after shooting, adviser says
stories.description: The first lady urged officials to postpone the White House Correspondents' Dinner even as President Trump publicly called for the "show" to go on.

stories.title: Iraqi supertanker appears to cross Strait of Hormuz amid near-standstill in traffic
stories.description: Ship-tracking data showed the Kin A off Oman's coast Saturday after going dark for three days, joining a handful of tankers to navigate the choked waterway.

stories.title: Iran accuses US of nuclear treaty violations at NPT review conference
stories.description: Tehran's UN mission defended its right to unlimited uranium enrichment and called Washington's nuclear posture "outrageous and hypocritical."

stories.title: USDA moves to close loophole letting luxury car owners collect food stamps
stories.description: Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins says the department is close to ending a policy that allowed 14,000 luxury vehicle owners in one state to receive SNAP benefits.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill dozens in Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.description: At least 44 people have been killed by Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon over the past two days, with both sides violating the April 17 truce.

stories.title: NYPD arrests protesters who blockaded NYSE on May Day
stories.description: More than 100 demonstrators tried to shut down every entrance to the exchange as part of nationwide "Workers Over Billionaires" actions.

stories.title: US warns EU allies of arms delivery delays as Iran war drains stockpiles
stories.description: The Financial Times reported Friday that warnings now extend beyond Baltic states to include the UK and Poland, as the conflict strains U.S. munitions reserves.

stories.title: Bard College president resigns after review of Epstein ties
stories.description: Leon Botstein, who led the college for 51 years, stepped down after investigators found he minimized his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

stories.title: Trump's 'Garden of Heroes' plans expand with pools, amphitheater, dining venues
stories.description: The latest blueprints could require a major redevelopment of West Potomac Park near the National Mall, the New York Times reports.

stories.title: More than 3 million lose SNAP as May 1 rules take full effect
stories.description: The cutoff marks the end of a three-month compliance window under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act's expanded work requirements.

stories.title: Iran demands six Arab states pay reparations over war
stories.description: Tehran's UN envoy rejected Gulf states' self-defense claims, calling their role in the U.S.-Israeli campaign acts of aggression under international law.

stories.title: Trump says Medicare patients to get GLP-1 drugs by July
stories.description: The Bridge program will offer Wegovy and Zepbound to eligible seniors for a $50 monthly copay, operating outside the standard Part D benefit.

stories.title: FEMA offers jobs back to workers cut under Noem
stories.description: The agency told a federal court it is reaching out to disaster workers whose contracts were not renewed in January, reversing cuts that sparked a lawsuit.

stories.title: HHS visa delays leave hundreds of foreign doctors facing deportation
stories.description: Physicians contracted to work in underserved U.S. communities may have to leave the country if waiver applications aren't processed before a July 30 deadline.

stories.title: Trump signs order expanding Cuba sanctions
stories.description: The executive order targets individuals and entities supporting Cuba's security apparatus amid an escalating maximum pressure campaign.

stories.title: Kemp rules out redrawing Georgia maps before midterms
stories.description: The governor said voting is already underway and he won't cancel the May 19 primary, breaking from GOP counterparts in other states.

stories.title: Ex-Rep. Rivera convicted in secret Venezuela lobbying case
stories.description: A Miami federal jury found Rivera and associate Esther Nuhfer guilty on all counts, including failing to register as foreign agents for the Maduro government.

stories.title: US to shut Gaza ceasefire center as Trump plan stalls
stories.description: The CMCC in southern Israel will be folded into a smaller international force, cutting the U.S. military presence from around 190 troops to 40.

stories.title: Exxon and Chevron refuse to boost output despite White House pressure
stories.description: Both oil supermajors reported sharp profit declines but beat adjusted estimates, doubling down on cash flow over production growth amid the Iran war.

stories.title: Iran accelerates weapons recovery as ceasefire talks stall, NBC News reports
stories.description: U.S. officials say Tehran is exploiting the truce to dig out hidden missiles and drones as the prospect of renewed conflict grows.

stories.title: Pentagon strikes AI deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and four other tech firms for classified work
stories.description: The Defense Department now has six major AI partnerships for classified military networks, following its bitter split with Anthropic earlier this year.

stories.title: Trump says he 'doesn't care' if Powell stays on Fed board
stories.description: A day after blasting the Fed chair on social media, the president struck a notably milder tone about Powell's decision to remain as a governor.

stories.title: Senate rejects House FISA bill, passes 45-day extension
stories.description: Lawmakers stripped a central bank digital currency ban that House Republicans attached, calling it a "poison pill" blocking surveillance renewal.

stories.title: UAE bans citizens from traveling to Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq
stories.description: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs urged Emiratis already in those countries to return immediately, citing regional security concerns amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

stories.title: Trump says US has 'already won' Iran war, vows to hold blockade until nuclear deal
stories.description: Oil hit wartime highs above $120 a barrel as the president rejected Iran's latest peace proposal and insisted on permanent nuclear guarantees.

stories.title: China launches combat patrols near Scarborough Shoal amid US-Philippines drills
stories.description: Beijing's military and coast guard conducted naval and air operations in the disputed South China Sea as the largest-ever Balikatan exercises continue nearby.

stories.title: Camp Mystic withdraws license bid to reopen after fatal flood
stories.description: The all-girls camp in Hunt, Texas, abandoned plans for summer 2026 after weeks of pressure from lawmakers, the lieutenant governor and victims' families.

stories.title: FEMA reinstates whistleblowers sidelined under Noem
stories.description: Fifteen employees who signed the "Katrina Declaration" return to work as the agency prepares for hurricane season and the FIFA World Cup.

stories.title: U.S. economy grew 2% in Q1 as jobless claims hit 57-year low
stories.description: Thursday's data painted a mixed picture: GDP rebounded but missed forecasts, inflation accelerated sharply, and unemployment filings plunged to levels not seen since 1969.

stories.title: Maine Gov. Mills drops Senate bid, clearing path for Platner
stories.description: The two-term governor cited insufficient resources to continue against the insurgent oyster farmer, who led by double digits in polling.

stories.title: Trump says WHCA dinner agent was shot by suspect, not friendly fire
stories.description: Ballistic analysis traced the round that struck the Secret Service agent's vest to Cole Allen's shotgun, ending days of speculation.

stories.title: US jobless claims drop to 189,000, near lowest since 1969
stories.description: The sharp weekly decline defies expectations of rising layoffs amid tariffs and geopolitical tensions, with filings falling well below forecasts.

top.title: Trump departs for Beijing, says U.S. needs no help to beat Iran
top.description: The president dismissed the need for Chinese intervention in the two-month-old Iran war as he left for a summit with Xi Jinping.

top.title: Altman testifies Musk sought control of OpenAI before departure
top.description: The OpenAI CEO told jurors Musk wanted to merge the AI startup with Tesla and pushed for long-term dominance over the organization.

top.title: WHO raises hantavirus cruise ship cases to 11, warns more likely
top.description: Spain confirmed a new infection among repatriated passengers as WHO said the virus's long incubation period means additional cases are expected.

top.title: CBO pegs Trump's Golden Dome at $1.2 trillion over 20 years
top.description: The estimate is roughly seven times the $175 billion price tag Trump cited when he unveiled the missile defense program last year.

top.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum rules out US extraction of indicted Sinaloa governor
top.description: The president said U.S. authorities will not be allowed to forcibly remove Governor Rocha Moya, who faces federal drug trafficking charges.

top.title: Starmer tells cabinet he won't quit as rivals circle
top.description: The PM challenged Labour critics to formally trigger a leadership contest, as Burnham, Rayner and Streeting jostle to succeed him.

top.title: Makary resigns as FDA commissioner; Diamantas named acting chief
top.description: HHS Secretary Kennedy made the final call after months of clashes over vaping, abortion policy, and drug approvals, according to Politico.

top.title: Pentagon raises Iran war cost estimate to $29 billion
top.description: The updated figure marks a $4 billion jump from two weeks ago, though independent analysts say the true cost is far higher.

top.title: US consumer prices jump 3.8% in April, fastest pace since mid-2023
top.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that annual inflation accelerated from 3.3% in March, driven by the Iran war's ongoing energy shock.

top.title: Senate confirms Warsh to Fed board, chair vote next
top.description: The Tuesday vote completes the first step toward installing Trump's pick as Fed chair before Jerome Powell's term expires Friday.

top.title: Iran redefines Strait of Hormuz as 500-km military zone
top.description: The IRGC Navy says its operational area now spans a "complete crescent" from Jask to beyond Qeshm Island, a tenfold expansion of the critical oil chokepoint.

top.title: Russia launches over 200 drones as U.S.-brokered ceasefire expires
top.description: Ukraine says Russia struck civilian targets including a Kyiv high-rise and a kindergarten overnight, killing at least one and wounding several.

top.title: Trump says Cuba is 'asking for help,' signals talks ahead of China trip
top.description: The president posted on Truth Social that the U.S. will engage in discussions with Havana, without offering details on timing or format.

top.title: Navy's 30-year shipbuilding plan opens door to overseas construction
top.description: The newly released strategy aims for 450 vessels by 2031 and seeks congressional authority to build some ships at allied shipyards abroad.

top.title: Pakistan calls CBS report on Iranian jets at airbase 'misleading'
top.description: The Foreign Office dismissed claims that Iran parked military aircraft at Nur Khan Airbase to shield them from U.S. strikes, citing routine diplomatic logistics.

top.title: China pressed Anthropic for access to its Mythos AI model and was refused
top.description: The approach at a Singapore meeting alarmed White House officials ahead of the Trump-Xi summit set for May 14-15 in Beijing.

top.title: Lebanon urges US pressure on Israel to halt raids as ceasefire frays
top.description: President Aoun and PM Salam pressed the U.S. ambassador to push Israel to stop strikes ahead of Washington talks this week.

top.title: CENTCOM says 62 ships redirected under Iran blockade
top.description: More than 20 U.S. warships are enforcing the naval blockade as Iran submits a 14-point counter-proposal to end the standoff.

top.title: Trump delays plan to suspend beef import tariffs
top.description: The administration postponed executive orders that would have waived tariff-rate quotas on beef for 200 days to help lower record-high prices.

top.title: Brazilian flotilla activist returns home alleging torture by Israel
top.description: Thiago Ávila arrived in São Paulo on Monday after 10 days in Israeli custody, claiming he and other detainees suffered widespread abuses.

top.title: Turkey set to receive first-ever US emergency oil shipment
top.description: Two tankers carrying nearly 1.8 million barrels of Strategic Petroleum Reserve crude are headed to Turkey amid the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

top.title: Navy confirms Trump-class battleships will be nuclear-powered
top.description: A new 30-year shipbuilding plan also reveals the Navy intends to buy 15 of the vessels, up from three previously announced.

top.title: Japan pushes for New Zealand to choose Mogami-class frigates over UK rival
top.description: Tokyo voiced hope Monday that New Zealand will select the upgraded Mogami-class frigate, which Australia has already ordered, over Britain's Type 31.

top.title: Iran says 35 million people face water shortages as crises mount
top.description: Officials warned Monday that drought, a U.S. naval blockade, mass layoffs, and an internet blackout are converging into a deepening domestic emergency.

top.title: Trump weighs military action as Iran ceasefire talks collapse
top.description: The president convened his national security team Monday after rejecting Tehran's latest proposal and declaring the truce on "massive life support."

top.title: Democrats demand Kiggans resign over racial slur response
top.description: The Congressional Black Caucus and Democratic lawmakers called for her ouster after she agreed with a host's racist remark about Hakeem Jeffries.

top.title: Pakistan let Iran park warplanes on its airfields
top.description: Islamabad quietly hosted Iranian military aircraft at Nur Khan Airbase while serving as the central mediator between Washington and Tehran.

top.title: Pentagon reveals nuclear submarine's location in rare message to Iran
top.description: The rare public deployment of a nuclear-armed submarine coincides with collapsed peace talks and Trump's rejection of Tehran's counterproposal.

top.title: House Democrats demand subpoenas over prediction market insider trading
top.description: Seven lawmakers asked Oversight Chair Comer to investigate suspicious Polymarket bets placed hours before U.S. strikes on Iran.

top.title: IDF sentences soldiers for desecrating Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
top.description: The sentencing marks the second punishment in weeks for vandalism of Christian symbols in the same southern Lebanese village.

top.title: Trump to undergo annual physical May 26 at Walter Reed
top.description: The White House announced the 79-year-old president's dental and medical evaluations amid months of public speculation about his health.

top.title: Kennedy quietly leading broad vaccine safety probe across health agencies
top.description: While publicly silent on vaccines ahead of midterms, the health secretary is directing government scientists to investigate his theories on vaccines and chronic disease.

top.title: EU's Kallas says Putin is 'weaker than ever' on Ukraine
top.description: The bloc also imposed new sanctions on 16 individuals and seven entities tied to the forced relocation of an estimated 20,500 Ukrainian children.

top.title: GOP splits over $1B security provision for Trump ballroom
top.description: Senate Republicans returned to Washington Monday facing internal resistance to a $1 billion taxpayer-funded security provision tied to Trump's White House ballroom project.

top.title: California mayor agrees to plead guilty to acting as Chinese agent
top.description: Eileen Wang resigned and will plead guilty to secretly promoting China's interests through a propaganda website with her then-fiancé.

top.title: Knesset passes law creating military tribunal for Oct. 7 suspects
top.description: The bipartisan legislation establishes a special court in Jerusalem to try hundreds of detained Hamas attackers, with genocide convictions carrying a mandatory death sentence.

top.title: Trump urges House to pass housing bill banning corporate homebuyers
top.description: The president demanded Congress approve the Senate's 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which restricts large investors from buying single-family homes.

top.title: House Ethics Committee issues 20-plus subpoenas in Rep. Cory Mills probe
top.description: The panel called allegations of dating violence, campaign finance violations, and military service misrepresentation "serious and complex."

top.title: Supreme Court clears Alabama to drop a majority-Black House district
top.description: The justices vacated a lower court order requiring two majority-Black congressional districts, sending the case back under a new, stricter legal standard.

top.title: Trump nominates Kari Lake as US ambassador to Jamaica
top.description: The nomination moves Lake out of her role at USAGM, where a federal judge ruled her appointment was unlawful and voided her actions.

top.title: MSF suspends operations in Haiti's Cité Soleil as gang clashes force hospital evacuations
top.description: The only trauma hospital in the densely populated Port-au-Prince district shut down Monday, leaving hundreds of thousands without medical care.

top.title: Virginia Democrats ask Supreme Court to restore voter-approved congressional map
top.description: The emergency appeal seeks to reverse a state court ruling that voided an April redistricting referendum projected to give Democrats four additional House seats.

top.title: Comey calls indictment evidence of Trump's 'bottomless desire' for revenge
top.description: The former FBI director spoke out Monday as his July trial on charges tied to a seashell Instagram post approaches.

top.title: Putin says war 'coming to an end' as Germany rejects his peace mediator
top.description: Germany dismissed Putin's proposal to use ex-Chancellor Schröder as a mediator, saying Russia has shown no signs of serious interest in negotiations.

top.title: Suspect in White House dinner shooting pleads not guilty as poll reveals widespread doubt
top.description: Cole Allen entered a not guilty plea Monday on charges including attempted assassination of the president, as a new survey found 24% of Americans believe the attack was staged.

top.title: Trump says he's 'seriously considering' Venezuela as 51st state
top.description: Venezuela's acting president rejected the proposal, saying "that idea would never have crossed our minds" while speaking at The Hague.

top.title: Iran threatens 'decisive' response to UK, French warships near Hormuz
top.description: Tehran warned Sunday that European naval vessels entering the Strait of Hormuz would be met with force, as Macron said the mission would be "coordinated with Iran."

top.title: Supreme Court extends abortion pill mail access until Thursday
top.description: The justices now have until May 14 to decide whether to allow a Fifth Circuit ruling reinstating in-person dispensing requirements to take effect nationwide.

top.title: Two-thirds of Americans say Trump hasn't justified Iran war, poll finds
top.description: A Reuters/Ipsos survey completed Monday also found 63% of Americans say surging gas prices from the conflict have hurt their finances.

top.title: Hassett rules out new agency for AI oversight
top.description: The statement walks back a week of speculation after the White House floated FDA-style pre-approval for AI models.

top.title: Iran's record internet blackout threatens mass layoffs as economic toll mounts
top.description: Bloomberg reports the 70-day-plus shutdown, the longest ever recorded in a connected society, is devastating private businesses alongside war damage.

top.title: Trump administration repeals Biden-era conservation rule for public lands
top.description: The BLM formally struck down a rule that had elevated habitat protection alongside drilling and grazing on 245 million acres in the West.

top.title: One Nation wins first federal lower house seat in Farrer by-election
top.description: David Farley's emphatic victory ends the Coalition's 77-year grip on the NSW electorate, marking a historic breakthrough for Pauline Hanson's party.

top.title: Global oil stockpiles draining at record pace amid Iran war
top.description: Morgan Stanley estimates inventories fell by 4.8 million barrels a day from March to late April, the steepest drawdown the IEA has ever recorded.

top.title: DOJ launches aggressive leak probes after Trump presses AG on Iran war coverage
top.description: The Wall Street Journal reports Trump privately pushed acting AG Todd Blanche to crack down on media leaks tied to the Iran conflict.

top.title: Israel built secret base in Iraq during Iran war, WSJ reports
top.description: The clandestine outpost housed special forces and served as a logistics hub, with U.S. knowledge but without Iraq's approval.

top.title: U.S. strikes third alleged drug boat in five days, killing 2
top.description: The campaign known as Operation Southern Spear has now killed at least 193 people since September, according to the AP.

top.title: Trump nominates fired FEMA chief Hamilton to lead agency again
top.description: The former Navy SEAL was dismissed a year ago after telling Congress that abolishing FEMA was not in the country's best interests.

top.title: Bahrain arrests 41 over alleged links to Iran's IRGC
top.description: The detainees are accused of espionage and sympathy with Iranian aggression, part of a broader crackdown since the U.S.-Israel-Iran war began.

top.title: Alito relied on misleading DOJ data to gut Voting Rights Act, analysis finds
top.description: The Guardian found the voter turnout statistics in the Supreme Court's landmark Section 2 ruling used a methodology experts call deliberately deceptive.

top.title: Trump says he'll raise Taiwan arms sales with Xi in Beijing
top.description: A bipartisan group of senators is pressing him to advance a stalled $14 billion weapons package before the May 14-15 summit.

top.title: US restates opposition to West Bank annexation at UN
top.description: Ambassador Jeff Bartos told a UN Security Council session that Washington opposes Israeli annexation, citing regional stability concerns.

top.title: Rubio calls Iran's claim over Strait of Hormuz 'unacceptable' as U.S. awaits deal response
top.description: The secretary of state warned from Rome that Tehran's new transit authority over the critical waterway sets a dangerous precedent.

top.title: Over 2,500 scientists urge Congress to reverse Trump's NSF board firings
top.description: An open letter calls the dismissal of all 22 National Science Board members an "alarming attack" on U.S. research funding and scientific independence.

top.title: Four men found guilty in assassination of Haitian President Moïse
top.description: A Miami federal jury convicted four men on all counts for conspiring to kill the Haitian leader, who was gunned down in 2021.

top.title: Pentagon releases 162 declassified UFO files to public
top.description: The first batch includes unresolved cases, Apollo mission photos, and infrared imagery, all accessible without security clearance at war.gov/UFO.

top.title: Trump says he plans to suspend federal gas tax
top.description: The president confirmed the move Monday, one day after Energy Secretary Wright said the administration was "open to all ideas" on fuel prices.

top.title: US adds 115,000 jobs in April, nearly doubling forecasts
top.description: The unemployment rate held at 4.3% as the economy posted back-to-back solid gains after months of volatile swings between job losses and gains.

top.title: Reform UK surges as Labour loses hundreds of seats in local elections
top.description: Starmer vowed he would "not walk away" as Labour lost control of at least eight councils and more than 235 seats in early English results.

top.title: Duffy defends reality TV show amid backlash
top.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy faces bipartisan backlash over a corporate-sponsored family road trip series filmed as fuel costs surged more than 50%.

top.title: Taiwan passes $25B defense bill for US arms purchases
top.description: The opposition-led legislature approved far less than the $40 billion the government sought, excluding domestically built drones and missile defenses.

top.title: Kremlin drafts plan to sell peace deal as victory, leaked documents show
top.description: Internal strategy documents task Russia's political bloc with reframing the war's end as a triumph amid economic strain and public fatigue.

top.title: Trump calls on Supreme Court justices to be 'loyal' to him on key rulings
top.description: The president singled out his own appointees Gorsuch and Barrett after a string of court defeats on tariffs and ahead of a birthright citizenship ruling.

top.title: Iran warns UAE will 'pay the price' after Qeshm blasts
top.description: IRGC-linked media accused Abu Dhabi of hostile action after explosions hit Iran's southern coast, while Tehran also alleged U.S. ceasefire violations.

top.title: US to start revoking passports of parents over unpaid child support
top.description: The State Department will begin revoking passports Friday for parents owing $100,000 or more, with plans to expand to a $2,500 threshold.

top.title: Biden to fight release of 70 hours of ghostwriter audio
top.description: A spokesperson confirmed Sunday that the former president will go to court to block the Trump DOJ from disclosing recordings tied to a classified documents probe.

top.title: CIA says Iran can withstand U.S. blockade for months
top.description: A classified assessment finds Iran retains enough economic buffers and military capability to endure the naval blockade for 90 to 120 days, the Washington Post reports.

top.title: New York reaches $268B budget deal with second-home tax
top.description: The agreement, announced five weeks past its April 1 deadline, includes a pied-à-terre levy on luxury homes and limits on ICE cooperation.

top.title: Trump eyes TikTok settlement funds for D.C. beautification projects
top.description: The administration is nearing a $400 million deal to resolve a child privacy lawsuit, with proceeds directed toward Washington improvements instead of victims.

top.title: ICE drops fast-tracked training, restores 72-day program
top.description: The reversal follows months of bipartisan scrutiny, whistleblower testimony, and a behind-the-scenes "handshake deal" with lawmakers during shutdown negotiations.

top.title: Judge unseals alleged Epstein suicide note after 7 years
top.description: The handwritten note, found by a cellmate weeks before Epstein's 2019 death, had been sealed in a courthouse vault and never shared with federal investigators.

top.title: US sanctions Chinese satellite firms over Iran war
top.description: Washington targeted three companies accused of providing imagery that enabled Iranian strikes on American forces, days before Trump's Beijing summit.

top.title: 8 in 10 Americans back age caps for Congress, poll finds
top.description: The NPR/PBS News/Marist poll found bipartisan support for both age limits and term limits, with Gen X expressing the strongest backing.

top.title: North Korea declares it is not bound by nuclear treaty
top.description: Pyongyang's UN envoy rejected the NPT at the ongoing review conference in New York, reaffirming the country's stance as a nuclear weapons state.

top.title: Judge strikes down DOGE's ChatGPT-led mass termination of NEH grants
top.description: The 143-page ruling found the ChatGPT-assisted cancellation of over 1,400 humanities grants violated the First and Fifth Amendments.

top.title: Ted Turner, CNN founder and media mogul, dies at 87
top.description: Turner, who had been living with Lewy body dementia since 2018, died Wednesday at his home near Tallahassee, Florida.

top.title: Iran's Ghalibaf vows nation 'will not surrender' amid US naval blockade
top.description: Parliament Speaker called for austerity and unity as the rial hits record lows and inflation surpasses 65% under the three-week-old siege.

top.title: Trump gives EU until July 4 to ratify trade deal
top.description: The president set the deadline after overnight trilogue talks on the Turnberry Agreement collapsed without a breakthrough.

top.title: North Korea drops reunification from constitution, codifies Kim's nuclear authority
top.description: The revised charter defines North Korean territory as bordering South Korea and formally places command of the nuclear arsenal in Kim Jong Un's hands.

top.title: China's Big Fund in talks to lead DeepSeek round at $45B valuation
top.description: The state-backed semiconductor fund's potential investment in the AI lab signals Beijing now views model capability as a strategic priority.

top.title: Trump Minneapolis immigration blitz hobbled gun, drug cases, Reuters finds
top.description: Federal criminal prosecutions in Minnesota fell sharply as Operation Metro Surge diverted thousands of agents from drug and gun investigations.

top.title: Democrat Greene wins Michigan Senate special election
top.description: The victory preserves Democrats' narrow state Senate majority during Gov. Whitmer's final months in office.

top.title: Rubio calls Israel-Lebanon peace 'imminently achievable'
top.description: The secretary of state identified Hezbollah as the sole obstacle to a deal, speaking at a White House briefing on Tuesday.

top.title: Boeing CEO expected to join Trump on Beijing trip next week
top.description: The planemaker is seeking its first major Chinese order in nearly a decade at the May 14-15 summit with President Xi Jinping.

top.title: Ramaswamy wins Ohio GOP primary, setting up costly governor's race
top.description: The Trump-backed entrepreneur will face Democrat Amy Acton in November after cruising past longshot challenger Casey Putsch on Tuesday.

top.title: US military confirms loss of KC-135 tanker over Persian Gulf
top.description: The refueling aircraft broadcast a 7700 distress signal and vanished from radar during Operation Project Freedom, the effort to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Rubio says Taiwan will come up at Trump-Xi summit next week
top.description: The secretary of state said neither side wants instability in the Indo-Pacific ahead of Trump's May 14-15 visit to Beijing.

top.title: Apple in talks with Intel, Samsung to make device chips in the US
top.description: The discussions could end a decade-long exclusive chipmaking partnership with TSMC as AI-driven supply constraints squeeze Apple's access to fabrication capacity.

top.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani skips Met Gala, spotlights garment workers instead
top.description: Rather than attending fashion's biggest night, New York's mayor published portraits of garment district workers whose labor makes the industry possible.

top.title: Judge lets Trump DOJ keep seized Fulton County ballots
top.description: U.S. District Judge Boulee ruled the FBI can retain more than 600 boxes of 2020 election materials taken in a January raid.

top.title: RFK Jr launches plan to curb antidepressant prescriptions
top.description: Kennedy unveiled the MAHA action plan at a Monday summit, targeting SSRI overprescribing with new clinician guidance and deprescribing reimbursements.

top.title: Iran's nuclear timeline unchanged despite war, US intel finds
top.description: Tehran can still build a bomb in about a year, the same estimate as last summer, despite two months of U.S. military operations, according to Reuters.

top.title: Trump-backed challengers oust most Indiana GOP senators who blocked redistricting
top.description: A majority of Republican state senators targeted by Trump over their December vote against his congressional redistricting push lost their primaries Tuesday.

top.title: ACLU files suit to block Texas migrant arrest law
top.description: The class-action lawsuit seeks emergency relief against SB 4 provisions that would let state police and judges enforce immigration law.

top.title: Putin retreating to bunkers amid coup and drone fears, EU intelligence report says
top.description: A drone struck a Moscow high-rise just 6 km from the Kremlin overnight, days before Russia's Victory Day parade.

top.title: FDA blocked studies finding Covid, shingles vaccines safe
top.description: Agency scientists were told to retract approved Covid vaccine safety studies and barred from presenting shingles research at a conference, the Times reports.

top.title: GameStop launches $56 billion bid to acquire eBay
top.description: CEO Ryan Cohen told The Wall Street Journal that GameStop has built a 5% stake in eBay and secured $20 billion in debt financing from TD Bank.

top.title: NATO's Rutte says Europeans 'got the message' on Iran war
top.description: The NATO chief spoke Monday after the U.S. announced it would pull 5,000 troops from Germany over allies' reluctance to support the Iran campaign.

top.title: Trump administration weighs pre-release vetting of AI models
top.description: The potential policy would reverse the president's hands-off approach to AI regulation, spurred by national security fears over Anthropic's Mythos model.

top.title: Starmer, Zelenskyy meet in Yerevan as UK seeks to join EU's Ukraine loan program
top.description: The British prime minister is pushing for UK defense firms to compete for contracts under the EU's newly approved €90 billion loan for Kyiv.

top.title: Competitive House seats hit historic low amid map wars
top.description: Just 16 tossup districts will likely decide control of the House, the fewest since Cook Political Report began ratings in 1984.

top.title: Trump's 'Project Freedom' launches with few details, leaving shippers scrambling
top.description: The U.S. began guiding vessels through the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, but shipping executives say the plan lacks coordination and clarity.

top.title: Bipartisan bill would cut university funds over foreign ties
top.description: Lawmakers introduced legislation targeting colleges with branch campuses or research ties to China, Qatar, Iran, Russia, and other adversarial nations.

top.title: Zelenskyy flags 'specific activity' on Belarus border
top.description: Ukraine detected unusual movements on the Belarusian side of its northern border on May 1, with Zelenskyy vowing to respond if needed.

top.title: DHS lifts visa processing hold for foreign doctors under travel ban
top.description: USCIS updated its website to restore visa processing for physicians from 39 banned countries after hospitals placed doctors on leave.

top.title: 2 US service members missing after exercise in Morocco
top.description: AFRICOM said the pair went missing Saturday near the Cap Draa Training Area during African Lion 2026, the command's largest annual joint exercise.

top.title: Democrats need four Senate flips in deep-red states
top.description: NPR's latest midterm analysis ranks North Carolina as the most likely flip, with Alaska as the potential majority-maker for either party.

top.title: Iran war gave China, Russia real-time look at US military gaps, WSJ reports
top.description: The conflict exposed American defense vulnerabilities, from THAAD radar disruptions to rapid depletion of critical missile stockpiles.

top.title: Melania Trump pushed to cancel dinner after shooting, adviser says
top.description: The first lady urged officials to postpone the White House Correspondents' Dinner even as President Trump publicly called for the "show" to go on.

top.title: Iraqi supertanker appears to cross Strait of Hormuz amid near-standstill in traffic
top.description: Ship-tracking data showed the Kin A off Oman's coast Saturday after going dark for three days, joining a handful of tankers to navigate the choked waterway.

top.title: Iran accuses US of nuclear treaty violations at NPT review conference
top.description: Tehran's UN mission defended its right to unlimited uranium enrichment and called Washington's nuclear posture "outrageous and hypocritical."

top.title: USDA moves to close loophole letting luxury car owners collect food stamps
top.description: Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins says the department is close to ending a policy that allowed 14,000 luxury vehicle owners in one state to receive SNAP benefits.

top.title: Israeli strikes kill dozens in Lebanon despite ceasefire
top.description: At least 44 people have been killed by Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon over the past two days, with both sides violating the April 17 truce.

top.title: NYPD arrests protesters who blockaded NYSE on May Day
top.description: More than 100 demonstrators tried to shut down every entrance to the exchange as part of nationwide "Workers Over Billionaires" actions.

top.title: US warns EU allies of arms delivery delays as Iran war drains stockpiles
top.description: The Financial Times reported Friday that warnings now extend beyond Baltic states to include the UK and Poland, as the conflict strains U.S. munitions reserves.

top.title: Bard College president resigns after review of Epstein ties
top.description: Leon Botstein, who led the college for 51 years, stepped down after investigators found he minimized his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

top.title: Trump's 'Garden of Heroes' plans expand with pools, amphitheater, dining venues
top.description: The latest blueprints could require a major redevelopment of West Potomac Park near the National Mall, the New York Times reports.

top.title: More than 3 million lose SNAP as May 1 rules take full effect
top.description: The cutoff marks the end of a three-month compliance window under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act's expanded work requirements.

top.title: Iran demands six Arab states pay reparations over war
top.description: Tehran's UN envoy rejected Gulf states' self-defense claims, calling their role in the U.S.-Israeli campaign acts of aggression under international law.

top.title: Trump says Medicare patients to get GLP-1 drugs by July
top.description: The Bridge program will offer Wegovy and Zepbound to eligible seniors for a $50 monthly copay, operating outside the standard Part D benefit.

top.title: FEMA offers jobs back to workers cut under Noem
top.description: The agency told a federal court it is reaching out to disaster workers whose contracts were not renewed in January, reversing cuts that sparked a lawsuit.

top.title: HHS visa delays leave hundreds of foreign doctors facing deportation
top.description: Physicians contracted to work in underserved U.S. communities may have to leave the country if waiver applications aren't processed before a July 30 deadline.

top.title: Trump signs order expanding Cuba sanctions
top.description: The executive order targets individuals and entities supporting Cuba's security apparatus amid an escalating maximum pressure campaign.

top.title: Kemp rules out redrawing Georgia maps before midterms
top.description: The governor said voting is already underway and he won't cancel the May 19 primary, breaking from GOP counterparts in other states.

top.title: Ex-Rep. Rivera convicted in secret Venezuela lobbying case
top.description: A Miami federal jury found Rivera and associate Esther Nuhfer guilty on all counts, including failing to register as foreign agents for the Maduro government.

top.title: US to shut Gaza ceasefire center as Trump plan stalls
top.description: The CMCC in southern Israel will be folded into a smaller international force, cutting the U.S. military presence from around 190 troops to 40.

top.title: Exxon and Chevron refuse to boost output despite White House pressure
top.description: Both oil supermajors reported sharp profit declines but beat adjusted estimates, doubling down on cash flow over production growth amid the Iran war.

top.title: Iran accelerates weapons recovery as ceasefire talks stall, NBC News reports
top.description: U.S. officials say Tehran is exploiting the truce to dig out hidden missiles and drones as the prospect of renewed conflict grows.

top.title: Pentagon strikes AI deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and four other tech firms for classified work
top.description: The Defense Department now has six major AI partnerships for classified military networks, following its bitter split with Anthropic earlier this year.

top.title: Trump says he 'doesn't care' if Powell stays on Fed board
top.description: A day after blasting the Fed chair on social media, the president struck a notably milder tone about Powell's decision to remain as a governor.

top.title: Senate rejects House FISA bill, passes 45-day extension
top.description: Lawmakers stripped a central bank digital currency ban that House Republicans attached, calling it a "poison pill" blocking surveillance renewal.

top.title: UAE bans citizens from traveling to Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq
top.description: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs urged Emiratis already in those countries to return immediately, citing regional security concerns amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

top.title: Trump says US has 'already won' Iran war, vows to hold blockade until nuclear deal
top.description: Oil hit wartime highs above $120 a barrel as the president rejected Iran's latest peace proposal and insisted on permanent nuclear guarantees.

top.title: China launches combat patrols near Scarborough Shoal amid US-Philippines drills
top.description: Beijing's military and coast guard conducted naval and air operations in the disputed South China Sea as the largest-ever Balikatan exercises continue nearby.

top.title: Camp Mystic withdraws license bid to reopen after fatal flood
top.description: The all-girls camp in Hunt, Texas, abandoned plans for summer 2026 after weeks of pressure from lawmakers, the lieutenant governor and victims' families.

top.title: FEMA reinstates whistleblowers sidelined under Noem
top.description: Fifteen employees who signed the "Katrina Declaration" return to work as the agency prepares for hurricane season and the FIFA World Cup.

top.title: U.S. economy grew 2% in Q1 as jobless claims hit 57-year low
top.description: Thursday's data painted a mixed picture: GDP rebounded but missed forecasts, inflation accelerated sharply, and unemployment filings plunged to levels not seen since 1969.

top.title: Maine Gov. Mills drops Senate bid, clearing path for Platner
top.description: The two-term governor cited insufficient resources to continue against the insurgent oyster farmer, who led by double digits in polling.

top.title: Trump says WHCA dinner agent was shot by suspect, not friendly fire
top.description: Ballistic analysis traced the round that struck the Secret Service agent's vest to Cole Allen's shotgun, ending days of speculation.

top.title: US jobless claims drop to 189,000, near lowest since 1969
top.description: The sharp weekly decline defies expectations of rising layoffs amid tariffs and geopolitical tensions, with filings falling well below forecasts.
